an4.60:1.1: Then the householder Anāthapiṇḍika approached the Blessed One …. The Blessed One said to him:
an4.60:2.2: "Householder, a noble disciple who possesses four qualities is practicing the way proper to the layperson, a way that brings the attainment of fame and leads to heaven. What four?
an4.60:2.3: "Here, householder, a noble disciple serves the Saṅgha of bhikkhus with robes; he serves the Saṅgha of bhikkhus with almsfood; he serves the Saṅgha of bhikkhus with lodgings; he serves the Saṅgha of bhikkhus with medicines and provisions for the sick.
an4.60:3.2: "Householder, a noble disciple who possesses these four qualities is practicing the way proper to the layperson, a way that brings the attainment of fame and leads to heaven."
an4.60:3.3:
When the wise practice the way
proper for the layperson, they serve
the virtuous monks of upright conduct
with robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines:
an4.60:3.4:
for them both by day and night
merit always increases;
having done excellent deeds,
they pass on to a heavenly state.
